text.title: System Design Process Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the system-design-process methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: NFRs MUST be numeric (RPS, p95 ms, % availability, GB/month). Adjective-only NFRs MUST be rejected and translated.
rule.rationale: Numeric NFRs are testable + driveable for capacity sizing; adjective NFRs cannot be validated.

rule.statement: Phase 2 MUST include a back-of-envelope capacity estimate (storage growth, query throughput, compute capacity) BEFORE design. Skipping the estimate produces architectures that fail capacity review.
rule.rationale: Numeric capacity bounds the design space; without it, designs default to over- or under-engineered solutions.

rule.statement: Design phase output MUST include at least C4 Level 1 (system context) and Level 2 (container) diagrams. Code-level diagrams (L4) MAY be omitted at this stage.
rule.rationale: L1+L2 capture the system boundaries and major deployable units — the level at which most architecture mistakes are made.

rule.statement: Every non-obvious decision in the package MUST land as an ADR with status, context, options, decision, consequences, and a review trigger. 'Obvious' decisions are those any peer architect would make identically.
rule.rationale: Implicit decisions accumulate as folklore and are lost when contributors leave; ADRs are institutional memory.

rule.statement: Phase 4 MUST include capacity sanity (computed RPS / storage fits the chosen design), anti-pattern scan (distributed monolith, premature scaling, missing observability), and peer review with at least one independent senior engineer.
rule.rationale: Self-review is unreliable; an independent reviewer catches blind spots and forces clarity.

rule.statement: When prerequisites hold (brief, stakeholders, constraints, current diagrams), run phases 1-5 in order. Skipping a phase is a process error.
rule.rationale: Each phase closes a specific failure mode; out-of-order skips them.

rule.statement: Do NOT use when (a) trivial CRUD module, (b) prototype / spike, (c) existing package current. Exit with note.
rule.rationale: Five-phase ceremony in these contexts wastes engineering capacity.
